title: quercus mongolica var. crispula
intro: Quercus mongolica var. crispula, also known as the Korean hand fern oak or crisp-leaved Mongolian oak, is a variety of deciduous tree native to East Asia, characterized by its distinctive crisp-edged leaves. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antiviral activities. The health benefits of Quercus mongolica var. crispula include reducing fever, relieving cough and asthma symptoms, and treating various skin conditions due to its antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties. In herbal preparations, the leaves and bark of Quercus mongolica var. crispula are commonly used in teas, infusions, and decoctions to harness its medicinal benefits.
